title: Peanut butter brownies
subtitle: Fudgy chocolate brownies with a peanut butter swirl
excerpt: Dense, fudgy chocolate brownies with a thick ribbon of peanut butter swirled through the top before baking. The brownie base is the standard melted-butter method: rich, intensely chocolate, with a crinkled top. The peanut butter is spooned on in blobs and dragged through with a knife just before the tin goes into the oven, creating a marble pattern that sets into a distinct peanut-chocolate layer.
sourceNotes: Brownies as a dense, fudgy chocolate bar baked in a tin and cut into squares are documented in American baking literature from the late nineteenth century. Fannie Farmer, The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book (first edition 1896, fully in the public domain and available at Project Gutenberg), includes an early brownie recipe. The peanut butter swirl variation is a well-documented home-baking adaptation appearing in American cookbooks from the mid-twentieth century onward.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze individually wrapped once fully cooled. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Brownies improve in texture on the second day as the crumb firms. Keep in an airtight tin at room temperature for up to 4 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 160°C. The brownies are done when the top is set and slightly crinkled and a skewer inserted 2 cm from the edge comes out with a few moist crumbs.

recipeTools.notes: 20 x 20 cm or 23 x 23 cm square tin, lined with baking paper.

glossaryTerms.definition: Brownies are done when the top surface is set (not liquid or wobbly when the tin is moved) and a skewer inserted 2 cm from the edge comes out with a few moist crumbs, not wet batter. The centre of the tin will still look slightly under-set at this point; it will firm as the brownies cool. A clean skewer from the middle means overcooked, dry brownies. Do not test from the very centre until the perimeter test passes.

content.text: Fudgy brownies depend on getting the bake time right. They will look underdone in the centre when the edges are set, and that is correct: they firm up as they cool.
Testing from 2 cm in from the edge
rather than from the centre is the reliable method. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; warm brownies tear and crumble.

Heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Line a 20 cm square tin with baking paper. Melt the chocolate and butter together in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and stir in the sugar. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla. Fold in the flour, cocoa powder, salt, and chocolate chips if using.

Pour the batter into the prepared tin and spread evenly. Drop spoonfuls of peanut butter over the surface and drag a knife or skewer through in a figure of eight pattern several times to create a marble effect. Bake for 22 to 26 minutes until the top is set and a skewer tested 2 cm from the edge comes out with a few moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before lifting out and cutting into 16 squares.
